All right, welcome back. So what I'd like to do now is show you how to make video ads that actually sell stuff and at the same time generate goodwill. You know, a huge part of online advertising is like getting people to know you like you and trust you, especially since we understand that most people won't buy from you immediately. And a great way to do that is video ads, specifically that actually deliver value. So I like to use this format to create video ads. They're designed to actually get someone to buy something or register for your lead magnet or whatever. But I also like these to be able to use the oldest trick in the book, which is to demonstrate you can help people by actually helping people. Because even the folks who don't register for your webinar or opt in for your lead magnet or buy your tripwire or whatever it may be, or schedule a call or what have you, those folks are your like anyone who watches these things, these are going to be your warmest audience. So regardless, we can still retarget them. Now one caveat on this, these ads, number one, they can be used for Facebook and YouTube ideally. So I like to make it where I don't have to make specific videos just for YouTube and specific videos just for Facebook. So that's thing number one. Thing number two is we always want to make these videos actual conversion ads, generally speaking, especially if we're using Facebook. So this isn't to be confused with content only ads that are run on Facebook just for videos or just for viewerships. You can retarget them later. These are really meant to get people to do stuff. And there's a framework. So what I'm going to do is I'm going to walk you through the framework. I'm going to give you this PDF as well. And then I'm going to splice in a very, very effective video ad that's been running forever. This made me a lot of money that follows this framework. All right? So you can have an example. You can do these direct to camera. You can do it with just audio and have B-roll overview or B-roll over audio or video sales letter style. It doesn't matter. But the first thing you want to do is you want to have your dog whistle intro. So just like our copy, when we have an image ad and we have copy above and below the image where we're just having like blatantly obvious who this is for. You want to do this with the video as well. So I literally come right out and say, hey, this is an ad and this is a video ad specifically for people who want to learn how to cook tomato pasta or whatever it may be. Now one of the reasons I do this is because when we use this for YouTube, if they go ahead and they skip the video, like right after that, if they skip the video in the first 30 seconds, you don't get charged for it. So regardless of the platform, whether it's YouTube or Facebook, we always want to be training their artificial intelligence to go after people who are perfect for us. So I really like to make my video ads very explicit in who they're for. So therefore in step number one, like literally first five seconds, you know, this is for people who want to achieve X result or whatever. And then if you can and you always should be able to do this, you want to sell them on watching the video. So I like to make my video ads educational. They don't have to be webinars or whatever, but the example I'm going to show you in here is a seven minute video ad and it sells $4 bundle. I like to make them actually beneficial to the viewer because it again, it uses that quote unquote oldest trick in the book, which would demonstrate you can help people by actually helping them. So what we want to do in this phase is say, hey, in this video, you're going to learn to three big secrets to having a well behaved dog no matter what kind of dog you have. And so that's, we're literally just telling them, here's why you should watch the video. So if we were to go from step one to step two, it would be like, hey, everybody, my name's Frank Kern and this is a video ad specifically for people who want to have a well behaved dog. In this video, I'm going to show you three big secrets to having a well behaved dog, even if your dogs like past a puppy phase, even if your dogs would chew and up everything in your house, even if you've tried and failed before. I'm going to show you how to train your dog today. All right. So that's all we're doing right there. All right. So like that whole little chunk, I'm still right around the 30 second mark there. And the reason I want to get points one and points two done in 30 seconds or less is because if they are not the right person, and this mainly applies to YouTube, if they're not the right person, especially for YouTube, they'll skip the video. And when they skip the video, they press that little skip ad button, you don't get charged. Right? Now, if it's on Facebook, you're going to get, you know, it's just you're paying per thousand impressions of the ad no matter what. So it doesn't really matter as much, but still you can retarget people who watch your video and you want to retarget the perfect people. Okay? So that's why we're doing this. That's why we're following this framework. Now our third step is if you can do this, you want to introduce yourself and establish credibility. Right? So in the example that I show you, I'm showing this video ad to complete strangers. They never heard of me. And I say, Hey, my name is Frank Kern. And you know, I've been doing this stuff since 1999 and I've helped over 80, 2000 people. Now if you're brand new, it might, you know, that might not be something that you could say. But you could certainly say, you know, Hi, my name is Frank Kern. And you know, I'm not a professional dog trainer, but I've got one of the most well-behaved and best dogs you've ever seen. I have a feeling that when you watch these, when you finish watching this video, you will agree with most other folks that will watch this video that, you know what, if I follow these tips and my dogs are going to be well behaved to, right? So you really just want to make that connection with them by no means should you inflate your credibility or credentials or anything like that. Okay. And so I think really anybody can do this. Doesn't matter what you're selling, even if we're selling makeup, you know, like, let's say I was a makeup artist. I'm going to pretend I'm my wife now. Actually, Natalia has too much real world credibility. So I'll pretend it's me. Okay. Like literally let's say I'm selling makeup. So I'd say something like, well, my name is Frank Kern and you probably wondering what in the world this great big ugly guy could tell you about skincare and cosmetics and makeup. And well, the funny thing is, is I'm the father of three daughters and I'm also married to a lady who just really is obsessed with her skin. And when I saw how important this was to my wife and to my daughters, I became obsessed with finding the proper ways that women could take care of their skin and really find a way that cosmetics could both help them look the way they want to look, but also keep their skin healthy and free of toxins. And that's what we're going to talk about in this video. Now I'm making all of that stuff up. I don't know a damn thing about skincare, but still it creates that bond with the audience. So I use that example because it's patently ridiculous. I know nothing about skincare, but still, you know, if that happened to be true, it would be perfectly acceptable. All right. So our next phase is we want to actually teach them something. And so as you'll see in the video ad that I'm going to splice into this, you know, so I really recommend you sort of look at the PDF version of this and then you watch the video at the same time, you can see where I'm doing it. You want to teach them three big things and you just want to give them big concepts. You don't want to overwhelm them. All right. So that section could be four minutes or something. It's not like you're doing a webinar. You literally just want to actually build desire by providing some results in advance here. Okay. So if we were selling makeup, that could literally be some tutorials on how to apply the makeup. If we're selling skincare or something, it could be like, hey, you know, here are three ways to make sure that you get rid of oily skin and no matter what age you are and no matter what your diet's like, and we teach them three big things around that, right? And then you transition into your pitch. Okay. And so in my pitch, in my video ad, you're about to see, I'm actually teaching them something and the last thing I teach them is you really need to have a good offer. And then I use that to transition. I say, for example, I have a really good offer for you today. Now you can get four complete marketing courses for $4. Another great way to transition into this is to literally say, regardless of what you're talking about, if you're really serious about skincare, for example, then blank and then you state the offer. If you're really serious about dog training, then blank. If you're really serious about having a successful online business, then blank. So if you're really serious about, is a great way to transition into the pitch. And then the next phase is you make the pitch. So in this part, we're not doing a full blown pitch like you do in a video sales letter or something. We just want to pre-frame them and get people who are interested to go to your website and let the website do the pitching. So in this case, let's say we're not even selling anything. Let's say that we just want people to go register for a webinar. We might say, if you're really serious about training your dog, you can click the link below and get access to a 30 minute on demand training that shows you five ways to take even the craziest dog in the world to a well behaved dog practically overnight. And so you're just telling them, here's what to do. Go click here and get this thing. And then you want to explain the benefits. It's not enough to say, okay, we have this on demand training or you can get a free trial of this newsletter or you can get four courses for $4 or you can get some makeup or whatever. We still want to explain the benefits. So let's use that dog training thing. In this case, we say, so the five things you're going to learn on this specific training for getting your dog to be well behaved are number one, number two, number three, number four, number five. And the reason you want to do this is because this will give you benefit one, benefit two, benefit three, benefit four, benefit five. And so that's it. It's taking 10 years. You know, we're just saying, here's why you want to go over here and click. All right. And then you want to give them that call to action again, which is, all right, you know, so that sounds good. You just go ahead and click the link below this ad and take me up on it. And so regardless of what we want them to do, whether it's opt in to get a free report, opt in to watch a webinar, you know, or buy four courses for $4 or watch your video sales letter or whatever it may be, this is the framework. And this framework works exceptionally well. The video that you're about to watch produces incredible sales for me all the time. And as you can see, it's, you know, it's following this framework exactly to the letter. And it's just me looking awful, just talking direct to camera to compolite strangers. And it's, it's working. So thus, without overcomplicating or anything, this is how I recommend that you build video ads. Because when you get these working, you're not only getting ads at work, but you're also building an audience of people who like you because you're actually helping them. All right. So what I want you to do is somewhere near here, you know, you can download the PDF of these slides and go ahead and open that up. And we're about to now segue into the actual video ad itself in three, two, Editing Magic 1. Here we go. Hey, my name is Frank Kern and this is an ad. It's an ad specifically for people who sell things online. So if that's you and you want to sell more stuff online, I think this can help you. In this ad, I'm going to do two things. I'm going to share three secrets with you that will help you sell more stuff online. And shockingly, I'm going to try to sell you something. This make good with the first promise, which is to help you sell more stuff online. So I got started in 1999 and since then as an advisor to internet entrepreneurs, I've worked with 82,000 customers. These are companies that have paid me to do one thing and one thing only, which is to help them sell more products and services online more consistently. And they're really three big secrets that I've distilled from working with all those people. I'll do it quickly for you. So here's secret number one, radical transparency. In today's internet age, people just don't trust ads. They don't trust people that they see online because there's a lot of, for lack of a better word, shenanigans. So if you are completely transparent with your intentions and what you're trying to do in your marketplace, they're going to trust you more. Trust breeds confidence. Confidence breeds sales. Take for example the fact that you're still watching this video. The very first thing I told you was that this was an ad and that I was going to try to sell you something, but you're still here. Why? Probably because of that transparency. I don't think it's because of my looks. Now the second big secret is this. Always, always, always try to make your ads actually help people, whether it's a paid ad or any type of marketing, try to make it help your client get the most important thing in the world and that is what they want. Our customers are prospects, whatever we want to call them, our marketplace. They're really not concerned with us. They're not concerned with our credentials. They're not concerned with whether or not we look cool or drive fancy cars. All they're ever really thinking about are three things. Number one, what they want. Number two, how they get it. Number three, what life is going to be like once they have it. So if your marketing actually shows them and genuinely helps them get what they want, what's going to happen? What trust is again going to increase. Trust breeds confidence, confidence breeds buyers. If they are confident that you can help them, they're going to be more likely to buy from you. So I recommend the oldest trick in the book, which is to demonstrate you can help them by actually helping them. It works pretty good. The third big secret is to make an irresistible offer. Make the way that you present your products so good that people just can't help but say yes, I'll give you an example. So you're watching this ad probably because you want to sell more things online. Here is an example of an irresistible offer. Right now you can get four complete courses about marketing showing you exactly how to do just that for $4. And this isn't like one of those tricks where it's $4 and then you get billed like a zillion times or whatever. Literally, it's four complete courses for $4. The first one is called the ultimate offer blueprint and it shows you exactly how to structure a presentation so that you can convey the value of what it is you're selling in a way that makes sense, is convincing, but isn't like high pressure or weird. Now this is a complete video course where I walk you through the creation of a presentation and I give you a workbook with fill in the blank slides so you can create your own along with me. So if you've ever wondered like how do I actually sell my stuff and if the idea of creating a sales letter or sales video like this one or a webinar or anything like that seems intimidating, the ultimate offer formula is going to cure that. Now the second course that I'm going to give you is meant to be taken after you complete that first one because it will make your sales presentation work better. It's called advanced persuasion tactics and in this training I share with you a method that's called close stacking. So here's what that means. Whenever you make an offer, say you say okay I have this thing and it costs X dollars and you can get it by doing this. Whenever you do that, what happens in your prospects mind is they start rationalizing whether or not they should make that purchase and they're really tuning you out in that moment. So if you just end your presentation by okay here's how much it costs and here's how you can get it, thank you. You're probably going to sell some but you're also going to be missing out on those people who are in analytical mode and they've tuned you out. But if you stay with them long enough, their brain will get out of analytical mode and it'll come back and it'll focus on you. So with close stacking, what I teach you how to do and this is in this advanced persuasion tactics training is I show you seven ways to restate and resell your offer in the same presentation. What this does is it compounds the power of the very presentation itself and makes it work better. The third training that you're getting, again all of this really is four dollars. The third training that you're getting is called power positioning. What this does is it helps you determine and control how your marketplace and your potential customers feel about you and what they think about you before you ever try to sell them something. The reason this is important is because if they already feel like they know you, like you and trust you, they're going to be way more inclined to buy from you when they finally hear your sales presentation or read it or whatever than if they didn't have that perception. This is the difference positioning, is the difference between companies like Apple and companies like Microsoft. Companies like Apple have raving fan customers. I'm one of them like super customers that'll stand outside to get the new version of the iPhone that looks just like the old version. And then you've got companies like Microsoft, cell computers. What's the difference between the two? It's positioning. So if you want to create raving fans instead of just customers, it's important to really get that positioning dialed in. That's what you learn how to do in that third training. The fourth training, and again you're getting all of these for four dollars. The fourth training is called the six magic steps to scaling big online. It's designed to help you with the three major areas of focus that you need to have in order to go from start up to six figures or from six figures to seven or from seven figures to eight and beyond online. Those three things incidentally are your thinking, your systems and your processes. And by the way, this has nothing to do with motivation or anything like that. This is more about what parts of the business really matter? Where do you really need to focus on? And the reason that's important is because when you have the right stuff that you're focusing on, it allows you to create systems and processes that let your business run without you. And that's how you get a business that will provide you and your family time freedom. So those are all the four courses that you're getting today for just four dollars.
